questions.text: Differentiate between acceptable risk and residual risk.
State the composition of lithosphere?
Explain physical vulnerability and ecological vulnerability.
Define disaster deficit index.
Explain psychosocial support related with disaster response.
List any six public health services required in responding to disasters.
Explain pyramid scheme related with participatory stakeholder engagement.
Define the terms counselling and crisis counselling.
Define man-made disasters and list examples from our country.
Explain the role of National Institute of Disaster Management in our country.
Illustrate with diagram the layers of earth’s atmosphere.
State and explain the classification of rocks.
State and explain the features of hydrosphere and biosphere in detail.
Explain the terms resilience, recovery and early warning systems with respect to disaster risk management.
Explain the applications of hazard maps.
Explain the methods of representing vulnerability.
Explain the method of expressing population risk.
List the components of risk assessment. Explain the contemporary approaches to risk assessment.
State and explain the types of disaster mitigation measures.
Identify the factors that determine the nature of disaster response and explain.
State and explain the types of disaster preparedness.
Identify the standard operating procedures to be followed during a disaster stage and explain.
State and explain the basic principles of participatory rural appraisal tools.
Explain the characteristics of effective crisis counsellors. State the advantages of crisis counselling.
State and explain the steps for effective communication.
Identify the barriers to effective communication.
List the global targets of Sendai framework and explain.
Explain the role, composition and responsibilities of National Disaster Management Authority.
List the guiding principles of Sendai framework and explain.
State the composition and role of National Executive Committee related with the National Policy on Disaster Management.
